## Summary

Adds the TrustedPath‚Ñ¢ action layer to the Revise Workbench. Time-pressed authors can now auto-accept all cross-check-approved Option A repairs in one pass instead of reviewing hundreds of individually verified repairs.

Only repairs with an `approve` verdict from the Perplexity cross-check verifier are eligible ‚Äî flagged, rejected, unavailable, and pending findings remain in the workbench for manual review.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `lib/revision/trustedPath.ts` ‚Äî New backend module: `applyTrustedPath()` queries approved verdicts from `revision_repair_cross_checks`, skips already-decided findings, and syncs `accepted_a` ledger entries via `syncRevisionLedgerDecisions`. `previewTrustedPath()` returns eligible count without applying.
- `app/api/revise/trusted-path/route.ts` ‚Äî New API route: `GET` for preview count, `POST` to apply.
- `components/revision/ReviseWorkbenchClient.tsx` ‚Äî UI additions: gold TrustedPath banner with eligible count, two-step confirmation flow, green success banner, auto-refresh of server ledger after application.
- `__tests__/lib/revision/trustedPath.test.ts` ‚Äî 17 tests covering the TrustedPath gate, verdict filtering, deduplication, metadata conventions, and title truncation.

**Does NOT touch:**
- `repairCrossCheck.ts` ‚Äî no changes to the verifier itself
- `ledger.ts` ‚Äî reuses existing `syncRevisionLedgerDecisions` without modification
- `FinalReviewClient.tsx` ‚Äî marked manuscript preview and color system are unchanged
- Database schema ‚Äî no new migrations (uses existing `revision_repair_cross_checks` and `revision_ledger_decisions` tables)
- The apply/export flow ‚Äî TrustedPath creates ledger decisions, the existing Final Review ‚Üí Apply flow handles manuscript mutation

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **Idempotent by design:** Re-running TrustedPath after some findings have been manually decided will skip those findings (`alreadyDecidedCount`).
- **No manuscript mutation:** TrustedPath only creates ledger entries ‚Äî the actual manuscript text replacement happens through the existing Final Review ‚Üí Apply pipeline.
- **Ledger entries are tagged:** All auto-accepted entries carry `metadata.source = "trustedpath-auto-apply"` for audit trail.
- **Two-step confirmation:** The UI requires an explicit Confirm click after the initial button press to prevent accidental bulk application.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed ‚Äî no evaluation pipeline code is modified. TrustedPath operates on the revision/ledger layer only.
